Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Answer Of Defendant Shumate Enterprises, Llc
Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jeremy Cloyd (415-988-9828) of Altair Law LLP for Plaintiff.
Dana Denno (559-433-1300) of McCormick, Barstow, Sheppard, Wayte & Carruth LLP for Defendants.

Hearing is held (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFs KENNETH KEISLER, KATE KEISLER's Motion To Strike Answer Of Defendant Shumate Enterprises, LLC is submitted, both sides to submit proposed orders. Plaintiff to submit proposed order granting motion to strike answer. Defendant to re-submit proposed order granting leave to intervene with reference to previous hearing date.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

***order granting Plaintiff's motion to strike answer of Defendant Shumate Enterprises, LLC is signed after hearing.***

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Answer Of Defendant Shumate Enterprises, Llc
Off Calendar Oct-05-2020 Continued To Nov-09-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs KENNETH KEISLER amd KATE KEISLER's Motion To Strike Answer Of Defendant Shumate Enterprises, LLC is continued to November 9, 2020. The Court grants plaintiff's request for judicial notice. Judicially noticeable facts show that defendant Shumate Enterprises, LLC is a suspended entity and the LLC concedes that fact. It therefore cannot defend this action.

The main purpose of the statutory suspension is to collect a tax and parties raising the issue are only "incidental beneficiaries" of that law. (See Color-Vue, Inc. v. Abrams (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1599, 1605; American Alternative Energy Partners v. Windridge, Inc. (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 551, 562; Cadle Co. v. World Wide Hospitality Furniture, Inc. (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 504, 512-513.) Accordingly, the Court determines that a continuance is warranted to allow the LLC to obtain a Certificate of Revivor.

Counsel for the Shumate Enterprises, LLC is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
